title: overlayfs error -13 when using uidmap/gidmap
body:
### Issue Description

Privileged containers fail to start with '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/…/mapped/…: -13' errors when using uidmap/gidmap

This seems nearly identical to #18435 which was reported fixed in 4.5.0. Also this seems to be independent of any volume mounts, as the error happens with no volumes specified.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

`podman run --privileged --rm --name ntopng --net=host --uidmap=101:996 --gidmap=102:996 ntop/ntopng:stable /etc/ntopng/ntopng.conf --community`
Host redis uid/gid is 996, mapping to uid 101/gid 102 in container. Mappings are for the purpose of mounting volumes, but even without the volume mounts it fails.

### Describe the results you received

`[78924.510510] overlayfs: failed to resolve '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/aafe441c1761b54134b42243710bbd32fe759ae950d9a9a8dbde8de809fc443b/mapped/0/l/BG55VW6OERWYDUAQIQUHPN5Y7S': -13`

### Describe the results you expected

Expected the container to start

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Kernel 6.12.22-amd64 on Debian Trixie

### Additional information

Without `--uidmap`/`--gidmap` the container starts correctly.

comments.body:
you've mapped only one user. 

You need to specify the mappings for the entire user namespace, try adding something like `--uidmap=0:1000:101 --gidmap=0:1000:102  --uidmap=102:2000:1000 --gidmap=103:2000:1000`

Probably you would like the other IDs to be automatically assigned, in that case you need `--userns="auto:uidmapping=101:906:1,gidmapping=102:906:1" `
